headline: CEO at BlueZoo, Inc.
summary: I love a great product, especially one that is well-engineered and creates an entirely new product category.  I was a part of the teams that brought to market the first RDBMS designed for data warehousing in 1992 (Red Brick), one of the first web content management solutions in 1997 (Aziza), one of the first enterprise e-commerce application in 2001 (Blue Martini), and one of the first audience (i.e. human occupancy) measurement solutions in 2020 (BlueZoo).  Innovative software can make the world a better place!

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description: BlueZoo provides audience and occupancy measurement services to the out-of-home advertising, retail, and insurance markets.  BlueZoo's passive Wi-Fi technology counts smartphones as a proxy for people.  Unlike GPS-based mobile apps, BlueZoo's technology is privacy friendly and measures 95% of smartphones in a designated detection zone.  BlueZoo's technology is protected by seven granted USPTO patents. Some of the world's largest OOH advertisers (JCDecaux), retailers (Home Depot), and insurers (The Hartford) use BlueZoo products.  Advertisers and retailers measure audience sizes of digital signage to set advertising prices.  Insurers measure occupancy as an indicator of risk, and seek to mitigate and correctly price this risk. Relevant to insurance markets, BlueZoo markets a simplified product, branded "Party Squasher", to short term rental (e.g. Airbnb) operators.  Party Squasher detects a party when people gather, typically more than 1.5 hours before the noise starts, and notifies the rental manager via SMS or email in real time.

experiences.title: Partner
experiences.description: Market Builders focuses on helping tech startups with market development including messaging, market validation, marketing collateral, sales tools, and demand generation.  Sectors include SaaS/cloud applications/infrastructure, storage, virtualization, and network security.  Scope includes both definition and implementation of marketing strategy.

experiences.title: General Manager, Business Storage Solutions Business Unit
experiences.description: Tasked with integrating and growing WD’s SMB storage group offering both WD network attached storage and Arkeia network backup appliances.  Focused on building a VAR / DMR / E-tail channel for software and appliances, complementary to WD’s channel for hard drives.  Defined product family of storage and backup appliances using a suite of custom-designed desktop and rack-based hardware platforms.  Responsible for SMB appliance revenues with team of 20 marketing staff. WD, competing with Seagate to be the world's #1 hard disk driver manufacturer, sought to diversify its revenue beyond storage hardware components.

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description: Arkeia Software delivered the first professional network backup solution for Linux in 1999.  Arkeia designed and sold backup software and appliances with 45% revenue from North America and 45% from Europe. Grew Arkeia Software by building marketing and restructuring sales to embrace VAR channel strategy.  Tripled revenue.  Refocused engineering on protection of virtual machine environments (e.g. VMware).  Acquired Kadena Systems in 2009 to own Kadena’s patented Progressive Deduplication.  Sold to Western Digital to expand their SMB business unit.

experiences.title: CEO
experiences.description: Parascale developed software for large-scale cloud storage.  Set go-to-market strategy, product definition, and company positioning.  Organized three beta sites for software and launched company and product to industry press.  Led Series-A search which culminated in $10M Series A round after four months.

experiences.title: SVP, International Business Unit
experiences.description: Created and implemented strategy for international expansion to replace existing eMachines and Gateway strategies.  Redirected business to concentrate on retail consumer markets. Focused on Japan and on major markets in Europe and Latin America, growing revenue by 18%. Introduced Gateway brand into Japan and eMachines brand into Mexico; negotiated terms for introduction of eMachines brand into Europe to set the foundation for follow-on growth.  Reported to CEO and was responsible for $270M in annual revenue. Managed staff of 35.  Gateway was sold to Acer in 2007.

experiences.title: SVP & GM Asia Pacfic
experiences.description: Launched Blue Martini in Asia-Pacific and acquired marquee accounts in each geography (e.g., Matsushita, Changi Airport, Metcash, and NTT) and developed partnerships with systems integrators (e.g., Accenture, IBM). Established master distributor network in Japan.  Established offices in Sydney, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Tokyo.

experiences.title: SVP Marketing
experiences.description: Defined and positioned product and company. Built strong brand on limited budget in a category crowded with established companies and start-ups. With revenues of $75M at IPO, Blue Martini became the fastest growing enterprise software company in history.  Blue Martini attained a market capitalization of $4B.   Employee #2. Hired and managed organization of 52 professionals, including teams for Corporate Marketing (PR, Marcom, Events), Product Management and Marketing, and Alliances (SI, ISV, platform). Launched four major product versions, recruited advisory board, launched user group, and built industry (e.g., retail, manufacturing) programs. Blue Martini Software (BLUE) delivered enterprise solutions for e-commerce, used by retailers and manufacturers. Blue Martini was incorporated in July 1998, funded by Matrix Partners and USVP in 1999, and completed an IPO in July 2000.

experiences.title: General Manage, Aziza Business Unit
experiences.description: Set strategy to build and sell a web content manager to the Global 2000, based on the Objectivity OODB.  Launched product in September 1997.  Aziza product was cancelled to focus on OODB OEM sales, but the web content management market blossomed with vendors like Vignette that launched the same year.

experiences.title: VP of Marketing
experiences.description: Selected OEMs and ISVs as the primary market for Objectivity's object oriented database, ending focus on enterprise markets.   Objectivity was funded by IVP, Mayfield Fund, Menlo Ventures and Sevin-Rosen, and ceased operations in late 2023.

experiences.title: Sales Representative
experiences.description: Number one salesrep for 1993 and 1994, achieving 166% and 132% of annual quota. In 1993, responsible for 70% of end-user licenses by value. Opened new markets, closing the company’s first customers in retail, distribution and manufacturing.  Closed first deal over $100k and trebled average deal size.  Closed Con Agra, DFS Group Ltd., The Good Guys!, Hewlett-Packard, Hughes Aircraft, Longs Drug Stores, Sysco, and Southern Pacific Railroad which became Red Brick’s core reference accounts.

experiences.title: Director of Product Management
experiences.description: Red Brick Systems developed the first RDBMS designed expressly for data warehousing.  Hired and managed staff of seven to define, price, promote the Red Brick Warehouse.

experiences.title: Senior Product Manager
experiences.description: Vendor of development environments for object-oriented languages (Smalltalk and C++).  Smalltalk was predecessor to Java.

education.description: National Science Foundation Fellow.  Thesis in MIT's Research Laboratory of Electronics, supervised by Professor Jonathan Allen, defined an NMOS DSP architecture for voice synthesis.  Taught courses in integrated circuit design to students from industry in the US (Boston) and Europe (Berlin) during two summers.

As President of Human Resources Management club, organized site visits to the coolest hi-tech manufacturing  facilities in Greater Boston (e.g. HP semiconductors in Hudson, GE aircraft engines in Lynn).  Summer job in non-consumer marketing at Sony in Tokyo, Japan.

Honors Program all of four years.  Enjoyed a stellar liberal arts education and still received a degree in electrical engineering.  First engineer at SCU elected to Phi Beta Kappa.  Graduated Summa Cum Laude.  Organized and led regular 42-person day trips by bus to Sierra skiing.
